_how_to_edit: This file controls the game's unit balance, treats economy, and AI difficulty.
Kids can safely change numbers here without touching the Python code.
Each unit in unit_key_order gets a number key on the keyboard: first unit = 1, second unit = 2, and so on.
sprite_style must be fast, strong, or tough because the current sprite sheet only has art for those three styles.
level_count is the total number of turf levels in that difficulty. Example: 4 means Level 1, 2, 3, and 4.
A level ends only when the AI side's turf health reaches zero.
ai_spawn_min and ai_spawn_max control how often the AI tries to spawn another unit.
ai_max_active_units controls how many AI units may be alive on the battlefield at the same time.
ai_stat_bonus_per_level is a percent increase per new level. Example: 0.15 means 15% stronger each level.
ai_regen_bonus_per_level is a percent increase to AI treat regeneration each level.
ai_spawn_delay_reduction_per_level is a percent reduction to AI spawn delay each level.

fast.label: Fast
fast.description: Cheap and quick. Good for rushing lanes.

weak.label: Weak
weak.description: Small cheap helper that swarms in numbers.
